tool_definitions.name: browser_snapshot
tool_definitions.description: Capture the current page as an accessibility-tree text snapshot. Interactive elements get [ref=eN] markers (e.g., [button] Submit [ref=e3]). Always call this before interacting with the page. Pass the returned snapshotId to every interaction tool — if the page changes and a ref becomes stale, you will receive a STALE_REF error telling you to re-snapshot. Loop: snapshot → act → snapshot (after DOM-changing actions).

tool_definitions.name: browser_click
tool_definitions.description: Click an element identified by its snapshot ref. Always pass snapshotId from the last browser_snapshot call — if the ref is stale you receive STALE_REF, meaning you must call browser_snapshot again before retrying. Clicking may change the DOM: re-snapshot if you need to interact with elements that appear or move after the click.

tool_definitions.name: browser_hover
tool_definitions.description: Hover over an element to trigger hover effects such as dropdowns, tooltips, and context menus. Always pass snapshotId from the last browser_snapshot call — a STALE_REF error means the ref is outdated and you must re-snapshot. Call browser_snapshot again after hovering if new elements appear.

tool_definitions.name: browser_press
tool_definitions.description: Press a keyboard key or key combination globally on the current page. Use for: Enter (submit forms), Tab (move focus), Escape (close modals/dialogs), ArrowDown/ArrowUp (navigate dropdowns and lists), Control+a (select all), Control+c/v (copy/paste), Shift+Tab (reverse focus). Key names follow Playwright conventions — single keys or combinations with Control, Shift, Alt, Meta separated by "+". Key presses may trigger DOM changes: re-snapshot if the page content changes.

tool_definitions.name: browser_type
tool_definitions.description: Type text into an element identified by its snapshot ref. Always pass snapshotId from the last browser_snapshot call — a STALE_REF error means the ref is outdated and you must re-snapshot. Typing may trigger DOM changes (e.g., autocomplete): re-snapshot if needed.

tool_definitions.name: browser_select
tool_definitions.description: Select an option in a combobox or listbox identified by its snapshot ref. Always pass snapshotId from the last browser_snapshot call — a STALE_REF error means the ref is outdated and you must re-snapshot. Selecting an option may trigger DOM changes: re-snapshot if needed.

tool_definitions.name: browser_fill_form
tool_definitions.description: Fill multiple form fields in one call. More efficient than calling browser_type/browser_select per field for multi-field forms. Always pass snapshotId from the last browser_snapshot call — a STALE_REF error on any field means the snapshot is outdated: re-snapshot and retry with fresh refs.

tool_definitions.name: browser_scroll
tool_definitions.description: Scroll the page in a direction, or scroll to bring an element into view. Provide ref to scroll to an element; provide direction to scroll the page. When using ref, always pass snapshotId — a STALE_REF error means the ref is outdated and you must call browser_snapshot first.

tool_definitions.name: browser_screenshot
tool_definitions.description: Capture a screenshot of the current page. Returns base64-encoded JPEG by default. Use browser_snapshot instead when the page has standard HTML/ARIA content — it is ~100x cheaper. Reserve screenshot for canvases, CAPTCHAs, or visual-only content.

tool_definitions.name: browser_wait
tool_definitions.description: Wait for a timeout duration, a selector to reach a state, or a navigation event.

tool_definitions.name: browser_tab_close
tool_definitions.description: Close a browser tab. Closes the current tab if tabId is not specified.

tool_definitions.name: browser_tab_focus
tool_definitions.description: Switch focus to a specific browser tab, making it the active page. After switching, call browser_snapshot to get refs for the new tab's content.

tool_definitions.name: browser_console
tool_definitions.description: Read buffered console messages from the current browser page. Captures log, warning, error, debug, and info level messages from the page's JavaScript console. Useful for diagnosing JavaScript errors and page-side issues without needing browser_evaluate.

tool_definitions.name: browser_navigate
tool_definitions.description: Navigate the current tab to a URL. Invalidates all refs from previous snapshots — call browser_snapshot after navigating to get fresh refs.

tool_definitions.name: browser_tab_new
tool_definitions.description: Open a new browser tab, optionally navigating to a URL immediately.

steps.message: Use the browser_snapshot tool to take a snapshot of the current page. Report what page elements you see.
